heading: Modification of certain limits for House candidates in response to personal fund expenditures of opponents
label: 30117 Modification of certain limits for House candidates in response to personal fund expenditures of opponents
notes: References in TextSubsections (a)(1)(A), (3), and (d), referred to in subsec. (a)(1), probably mean subsections (a)(1)(A), (3), and (d) of .section 30116 of this title
CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Congress, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 441a–1 of Title 2

children.label: A
children.text: the limit under subsection (a)(1)(A) with respect to the candidate shall be tripled;1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: the limit under subsection (a)(3) shall not apply with respect to any contribution made with respect to the candidate if the contribution is made under the increased limit allowed under subparagraph (A) during a period in which the candidate may accept such a contribution; and1

children.label: C
children.text: the limits under subsection (d) with respect to any expenditure by a State or national committee of a political party on behalf of the candidate shall not apply.1

children.heading: Determination of opposition personal funds amount
children.label: 2 Determination of opposition personal funds amount
i
children.text: the greatest aggregate amount of expenditures from personal funds (as defined in subsection (b)(1)) that an opposing candidate in the same election makes; over

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate amount of expenditures from personal funds made by the candidate with respect to the election.

children.heading: Special rule for candidate’s campaign funds
children.label: B Special rule for candidate’s campaign funds

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: For purposes of determining the aggregate amount of expenditures from personal funds under subparagraph (A), such amount shall include the gross receipts advantage of the candidate’s authorized committee.

children.label: I
children.text: the aggregate amount of 50 percent of gross receipts of a candidate’s authorized committee during any election cycle (not including contributions from personal funds of the candidate) that may be expended in connection with the election, as determined on June 30 and December 31 of the year preceding the year in which a general election is held, over

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate amount of 50 percent of gross receipts of the opposing candidate’s authorized committee during any election cycle (not including contributions from personal funds of the candidate) that may be expended in connection with the election, as determined on June 30 and December 31 of the year preceding the year in which a general election is held.

children.heading: Time to accept contributions under increased limit
children.label: 3 Time to accept contributions under increased limit
i
children.text: until the candidate has received notification of the opposition personal funds amount under subsection (b)(1); and

children.label: ii
children.text: to the extent that such contribution, when added to the aggregate amount of contributions previously accepted and party expenditures previously made under the increased limits under this subsection for the election cycle, exceeds 100 percent of the opposition personal funds amount.

children.heading: Effect of withdrawal of an opposing candidate
children.label: B Effect of withdrawal of an opposing candidate
children.text: A candidate and a candidate’s authorized committee shall not accept any contribution and a party shall not make any expenditure under the increased limit after the date on which an opposing candidate ceases to be a candidate to the extent that the amount of such increased limit is attributable to such an opposing candidate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The aggregate amount of contributions accepted by a candidate or a candidate’s authorized committee under the increased limit under paragraph (1) and not otherwise expended in connection with the election with respect to which such contributions relate shall, not later than 50 days after the date of such election, be used in the manner described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Return to contributors
children.label: B Return to contributors
children.text: A candidate or a candidate’s authorized committee shall return the excess contribution to the person who made the contribution.

children.heading: Notification of expenditures from personal funds
children.label: b Notification of expenditures from personal funds

children.heading: Definition of expenditure from personal funds
children.label: A Definition of expenditure from personal funds
i
children.text: an expenditure made by a candidate using personal funds; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a contribution or loan made by a candidate using personal funds or a loan secured using such funds to the candidate’s authorized committee.

children.heading: Declaration of intent
children.label: B Declaration of intent
children.text: Not later than the date that is 15 days after the date on which an individual becomes a candidate for the office of Representative in, or Delegate or Resident Commissioner to, the Congress, the candidate shall file a declaration stating the total amount of expenditures from personal funds that the candidate intends to make, or to obligate to make, with respect to the election that will exceed $350,000.

children.heading: Initial notification
children.label: C Initial notification
children.text: Not later than 24 hours after a candidate described in subparagraph (B) makes or obligates to make an aggregate amount of expenditures from personal funds in excess of $350,000 in connection with any election, the candidate shall file a notification.

children.heading: Additional notification
children.label: D Additional notification
children.text: After a candidate files an initial notification under subparagraph (C), the candidate shall file an additional notification each time expenditures from personal funds are made or obligated to be made in an aggregate amount that exceeds $10,000. Such notification shall be filed not later than 24 hours after the expenditure is made.

children.label: i
children.text: the name of the candidate and the office sought by the candidate;

children.label: ii
children.text: the date and amount of each expenditure; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the total amount of expenditures from personal funds that the candidate has made, or obligated to make, with respect to an election as of the date of the expenditure that is the subject of the notification.

children.label: i
children.text: the Commission; and

children.label: ii
children.text: each candidate in the same election and the national party of each such candidate.

children.heading: Notification of disposal of excess contributions
children.label: 2 Notification of disposal of excess contributions
children.text: In the next regularly scheduled report after the date of the election for which a candidate seeks nomination for election to, or election to, Federal office, the candidate or the candidate’s authorized committee shall submit to the Commission a report indicating the source and amount of any excess contributions (as determined under subsection (a)) and the manner in which the candidate or the candidate’s authorized committee used such funds.

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: 3 Enforcement
children.text: For provisions providing for the enforcement of the reporting requirements under this subsection, see .section 30109 of this title
